evals.prompt:
I've got three claims from my research that I need to verify against their sources. Can you submit them and then check them?

1. "OpenAI's GPT-4 was released in March 2023" - source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GPT-4 title: "GPT-4 Wikipedia"
2. "Anthropic was founded in 2021 by former OpenAI members" - source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anthropic title: "Anthropic Wikipedia"
3. "The transformer architecture was introduced in the paper Attention Is All You Need published in 2017" - source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transformer_(deep_learning_model) title: "Transformer Wikipedia"
evals.expected_output: The skill should submit all 3 claims, then verify them by dispatching claim-verifier agents (grouped by unique source URLs), and present a verification summary showing which claims verified clean and which had deviations.

assertions.name: claims-created
assertions.description: All 3 claims appear in claims.json with unique IDs and correct statements

assertions.name: workspace-initialized
assertions.description: claims/ directory structure created with claims.json, sources/, and history/ subdirectories

assertions.name: verification-attempted
assertions.description: Verification was dispatched — evidence of WebFetch calls or agent dispatches for the Wikipedia URLs

assertions.name: summary-presented
assertions.description: A verification summary was shown indicating how many claims verified, deviated, or had unavailable sources

evals.prompt: what's the status of my claims? I submitted some earlier and want to see what needs attention
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this as a dashboard request, read the claims.json, and render a properly formatted dashboard showing claims grouped by status with action hints for deviated claims.

assertions.name: mode-inferred
assertions.description: Correctly interpreted 'what's the status' as dashboard mode without confusion or asking for clarification

assertions.name: grouped-by-status
assertions.description: Claims displayed grouped by status categories (deviated, verified, unverified, etc.)

assertions.name: action-hints
assertions.description: Deviated claims include actionable next-step hints like 'use /claims inspect' or 'use /claims resolve'

assertions.name: formatting-quality
assertions.description: Dashboard uses markdown tables with claim IDs, truncated statements, and status information

evals.prompt: I have a claims.json with some deviated claims. Can you show me the details on the quantum computing claim and help me resolve it? The claim ID is claim-c3d4e5f6-a7b8-4c9d-0e1f-2a3b4c5d6e7f
evals.expected_output: The skill should first inspect the claim (showing deviation details, type, severity, source excerpt), then transition to resolve mode presenting the user with resolution options via AskUserQuestion.

files.description: Pre-populated claims registry with the sample data from the plugin's examples

assertions.name: correct-claim-found
assertions.description: Located the quantum computing claim by its ID and displayed the correct statement

assertions.name: deviation-details-shown
assertions.description: Shows the deviation type (data_staleness), severity (medium), source excerpt, and explanation

assertions.name: evidence-included
assertions.description: The verbatim source excerpt is shown before asking the user to make a resolution decision

assertions.name: resolution-options-presented
assertions.description: All 5 resolution options (correct, dispute, alternative source, discard, accept as-is) are documented
